line_items: 25 Engineering seats (Growth) @ €7,400/seat/yr; 25 Marketing seats (Marketing Ops) @ €6,480/seat/yr; 5% loyalty discount applied
notes: Initial Athena Cloud contract. Single PO covering both Engineering and Marketing departments under the Technology cost center. Approved by Sarah Chen (VP Engineering at time of signing). James Park noted: 'Simple single PO for now. If departments need separate budget tracking, we'll split later.'

line_items: 25 Engineering seats (Enterprise) @ €9,800/seat/yr; 12 Marketing seats (Growth) @ €7,400/seat/yr; 5% loyalty discount applied
notes: Year 2 renewal. Engineering upgraded to Enterprise tier. Marketing reduced from 25 to 12 seats. Margaret Walsh (CFO) required as approver for all vendor POs above €50K — first PO she personally approved for Athena Cloud. Margaret's comment during approval: 'The engineering utilization justifies this. The marketing seats need review in 6 months.' James noted that Margaret reviewed every line item and asked why marketing still had 12 seats when utilization was 48%.
amendment_reason: Year 2 renewal. Engineering upgrade to Enterprise. Marketing seat reduction (25→12).

line_items: 25 Engineering seats (Enterprise) @ €9,800/seat/yr; 12 Marketing seats (Growth) @ €7,400/seat/yr; 5% loyalty discount applied
notes: PO amendment: validity extended per contract auto-renewal (Year 3). Same terms. Margaret approved but added a note: 'Renewing under existing terms. I expect a formal vendor review in Q1 2026 including utilization audit and competitive benchmarking. Next renewal will not be automatic.' James forwarded Margaret's note to Elena (then AM).
amendment_reason: Contract auto-renewal. Validity extended to Oct 2026. Terms unchanged.

line_items: 25 Engineering seats (Enterprise) @ €9,800/seat/yr; 5% loyalty discount; engineering share = 75% of total monthly invoice
notes: NEW: Department-specific PO per Margaret Walsh's directive (Jan 2026). Engineering department now pays from its own budget (CC-4210-ENG) rather than the combined Technology cost center. Approved by David Wu as department head. David approved immediately — 'This is the most valuable tool my team uses. Happy to own the budget line.' Monthly amount = 75% of total invoice (€18,583.58 × 0.75 = €13,937.69). Discounted months (e.g., SLA credits) will be split proportionally. James instructed Athena Cloud (Tomás Herrera, new AM) to expect two separate SEPA transfers per invoice.
amendment_reason: CFO-mandated department-level vendor cost tracking. Engineering budget separated from combined Technology cost center.

line_items: 12 Marketing seats (Growth) @ €7,400/seat/yr; 5% loyalty discount; marketing share = 25% of total monthly invoice
notes: NEW: Department-specific PO per Margaret Walsh's directive. Marketing department now pays from its own budget (CC-4220-MKT). Lisa Huang signed as department approver but added a handwritten note on the approval form: 'Approving under protest. We are paying for 12 seats and only 5 are active. I have raised this issue multiple times (Nov 2024 email, support tickets TKT-4903, TKT-4909, Aug 2025 voicemail to Elena). Nobody has addressed it. I am approving this PO to avoid disrupting Engineering but I want it on record that Marketing is overpaying by approximately €3,100/month for unused seats.' James scanned Lisa's note and forwarded to Margaret with comment: 'FYI — Lisa raises a valid point. This should be addressed in the Q1 vendor review.' Margaret replied: 'Noted. Add to the review agenda.'
amendment_reason: CFO-mandated department-level vendor cost tracking. Marketing budget separated. Lisa Huang's protest note attached regarding unused seat overpayment.
